questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the Proton H2 Safety Orientation?

options.text: To provide employees and contractors with knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities, and Safe Work Practices that apply to all Proton H2 locations.
To introduce new employees to Proton H2's product line and business operations.
To fulfill a one-time government audit requirement for Proton H2 sites.

questions.rationale: As stated in blk_01_03, the orientation's purpose is to give employees and contractors knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities, and Safe Work Practices applicable to all Proton H2 locations.

questions.stem: Which of the following is one of the five expected outcomes upon completing this orientation?

options.text: Complete an approved first-aid certification course.
Access provincial OHS legislations.
Memorize all emergency evacuation routes for every Proton H2 site.
Obtain a valid operator's licence for site equipment.

questions.rationale: According to blk_01_06, one of the five listed outcomes is the ability to access provincial OHS legislations.

questions.stem: Workers have the right to a safe and healthy workplace, and this orientation will help them locate Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy.

options.text: True
False

questions.rationale: blk_01_02 states that everyone has the right to a safe and healthy workplace, and blk_01_06 lists locating and referencing Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy as an orientation outcome.

questions.stem: Which statement best describes Proton H2's expectation of all employees regarding health and safety?

options.text: Only supervisors and managers are accountable for health and safety performance.
All employees must follow health and safety guidelines as set out in the safety manual, and everyone is accountable for their own health and safety performance.
Employees are encouraged, but not required, to follow the safety manual guidelines.

questions.rationale: blk_02_02 states all employees are expected to follow the health and safety guidelines in the safety manual, and blk_02_03 confirms that everyone is accountable for their health and safety performance.

questions.stem: A worker witnesses a near miss on site but believes it was minor and no one was hurt. What should they do according to Proton H2's incident reporting requirements?

options.text: Wait until the end of the shift and mention it informally to a coworker.
Document it in a personal notebook and review it at the next team meeting.
Report it immediately, as all accidents, incidents, and near misses must be reported and investigated.
Only report it if a supervisor asks about any unusual events.

questions.rationale: blk_02_04 explicitly states that all accidents, incidents, and near misses must be reported immediately and will be investigated, regardless of perceived severity.

questions.stem: Where can the Proton H2 Health, Safety and Environmental (HSE) Policy be found?

options.text: At the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual.
In the appendix of the Employee Handbook.
Posted exclusively on the company intranet, not available in print.

questions.rationale: blk_02_09 states that the Proton H2 HSE Policy can be found at the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual.

questions.stem: Which of the following is an HSE commitment identified by Proton H2 in support of its Health, Safety and Environmental Policy?

options.text: Limit safety training to supervisors and managers only.
Comply with all applicable HSE legislation.
Defer hazard elimination until an annual safety review.

questions.rationale: blk_02_08 lists 'Comply with all applicable HSE legislation' as one of Proton H2's identified HSE commitments.

questions.stem: Which hazard control type sits at the top of the hierarchy and involves completely removing the hazard?

options.text: Administrative
Substitution
Elimination
PPE

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 defines Elimination as eliminating the hazard completely, placing it at the top of the hierarchy of controls described in the risk assessments module.

questions.stem: A site supervisor installs a physical guard around a piece of rotating machinery to protect workers from contact. Which type of hazard control does this represent?

options.text: Administrative control
Engineering control
PPE control
Substitution control

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 and blk_03_03 describe Engineering controls as those that address the hazard through enclosures, guards, barriers, or different tools/equipment, which matches the installation of a physical guard.

questions.stem: Under provincial OH&S legislation, what is the employer's primary responsibility regarding workers on applicable worksites?

options.text: To provide workers with the highest possible wage to compensate for workplace risks.
To do everything practicable to ensure the health, safety, and welfare of all workers, and to ensure workers are aware of their rights and duties.
To conduct a safety inspection only when a formal complaint is filed.

questions.rationale: blk_04_02 states that provincial legislation requires owners/employers to do everything practicable to ensure the health, safety, and welfare of all workers, and to ensure workers are aware of their rights and duties.

questions.stem: Workers are responsible for understanding relevant OH&S legislation and taking reasonable care to protect the health and safety of all workers and others in the vicinity of Proton H2 work.

options.text: True
False

questions.rationale: blk_04_03 states that workers must understand relevant OH&S legislation and take reasonable care and attention to protect the health and safety of all workers and others in or near the vicinity of work being done by Proton H2 and its contractors.

questions.stem: What number should a worker call in the event of an emergency involving a Proton H2 site?

options.text: (403) 000 0000 — the head office telephone number.
1 (877) 000 0000 — the Proton H2 24-hour emergency number.
There is no dedicated emergency number; workers should call their direct supervisor's personal cell phone.

questions.rationale: blk_05_02 and blk_05_03 identify 1 (877) 000 0000 as the Proton H2 24-hour emergency number to be used in the event of an emergency.
